gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewer found one helper metadata consistency issue; the branch now fixes it and adds a regression test. Re-review approved with no remaining findings.
verification: poetry run pytest -q tests/test_benchmark_runner.py tests/test_benchmarks.py tests/test_openai_compatible.py -> 61 passed

poetry run python -m document_kv_cache.repository_hygiene --repository-root . --output-json /tmp/document-kv-source-document-hygiene-final.json -> ok=true, forbidden_tracked_paths=[], forbidden_untracked_paths=[], dirty_tracked_paths=[], untracked_path_count=0
GPT-5.5 reviewer approved branch source-document-input-validation after the metadata helper finding was fixed
what_changed: Normalized SourceChunk chunk_id, chunk_type, and metadata at construction time.
Rejected non-string SourceChunk text and malformed SourceChunk metadata before cache generation.
Normalized SourceDocument chunks to a tuple and rejected empty or non-SourceChunk chunk collections.
Validated SourceDocument metadata and SourceDocument.from_texts chunk mapping inputs.
why: Source documents and chunks are public workflow inputs. Validating them before generation keeps malformed IDs, chunk types, and metadata from reaching manifests, KV keys, or benchmark prompts with harder-to-debug failures.
